mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) when either series reverts to mean.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle expansions, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which expands consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of observations, or the predictive IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined demand signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >70% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of stable monetary policy.
